zh.lifeSummary: 當Margery Alice Squire於1505年出生在英國薩默塞特巴頓 聖 大衛時，她的父親John Squire26歲，母親Lady Marguerite Capet de Bourbon21歲。她於1529年與John Adams在英國英格蘭薩默塞特巴頓結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1567年6月12日在英國英格蘭薩默塞特巴頓 聖 大衛享年62歲，埋葬在英國薩默塞特巴頓。

nameMeanings.name: Squire
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status name, occupational name, or a nickname from Middle English squyer, squer, skier, sker, northern Middle English swire (Old French escuyer, escuier) ‘shield bearer, esquire, personal attendant to a knight; landowner below the rank of knight; young man of gentle birth; household attendant, retainer, page, messenger’. Many early bearers of this name were of modest means and cannot have been members of the gentry; in such cases the name may have been used hyperbolically for a low-ranking servant or as a nickname. By the 17th century, the term denoted any member of the landed gentry, but this is unlikely to have influenced the development of the surname.

variants.name: Swire
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): variant of   Squire  , from a northern form of the word.

variants.name: Quire
meanings: Scottish or Irish: shortened form of   McQuire   (see   McGuire  ).

variants.name: Squires
meanings: English: variant of Squire , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Scuderi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carmelo, Salvatore, Angelo, Carlo, Gaetano, Vito, Antonio, Dante, Mario, Rocco, Rosario, Antonino, Biaggio, Eduardo, Ermelinda, Margarita.

Italian (Sicily): status name equivalent to Squire , from scudiero, scutiero, dialect scuteri ‘shieldbearer’ (from medieval Latin scutarius, an agent derivative of scutum ‘shield’). In Byzantium, the Greek word skoutarios denoted a member of the imperial guard.

variants.name: Square
meanings: English (Devon) and Scottish (Lanarkshire): variant of   Squire  .

nameMeanings.name: Margery
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual medieval vernacular form of   Margaret   (now also commonly spelled   Marjorie  ). This form of the name is preserved in the nursery rhyme ‘See-saw, Margery Daw’.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Margery Alice Squire was born in 1505, in Barton St David, Somerset, England, her father, John Squire, was 26 and her mother, Lady Marguerite Capet de Bourbon, was 21. She married John Adams in 1529, in Barton, Somerset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 12 June 1567, in Barton St David, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 62, and was buried in Barton, Somerset, England.

Elle a épousé John Adams en 1529, à Barton, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 5 filles. ko.lifeSummary: Margery Alice Squire(이)가 1505년 영국 서머세트 바턴 스트 데이비드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Squire(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Marguerite Capet de Bourbon(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1529년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 바턴에서 John Adams(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1567년 6월 12일 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 바턴 스트 데이비드에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 서머세트 바턴에 묻혔다.

ru.Barton St David, Somersetshire, England: бартон ст дэвид, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margery Alice Squire родилась в 1505 г. в местности бартон ст дэвид, сомирсет, Англия, её отцу по имени John Squire было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Marguerite Capet de Bourbon, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Adams в 1529 г. в местности бартон, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 12 июня 1567 г. в местности бартон ст дэвид, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 62 лет и была похоронена в местности бартон, сомирсет, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margery Alice Squire nació en 1505, en Barton St David, Somerset, Inglaterra, su papá, John Squire, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Lady Marguerite Capet de Bourbon, tenía 21 años. Se casó con John Adams en 1529, en Barton, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 12 de junio de 1567, en Barton St David, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultada en Barton, Somerset, Inglaterra.
